meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Pricing Strategy for Freelancers: Overcome Project Stagnation
content.seo_description: Stop stalling on client projects. Master SaaS pricing strategies and leverage Antigravity and Claude Code to ship profitable apps faster than ever before.
content.answer_block: The root cause of unfinished projects is often 'scope creep' masked as feature perfectionism. By implementing tiered SaaS pricing, you force a clear definition of MVP value. Use Antigravity to scaffold the architecture and Claude Code to automate the implementation, ensuring you hit milestones that satisfy both the client and your bottom line.
content.intro:
The graveyard of freelance projects is littered with 'almost-finished' apps that died because the scope expanded into infinity. You start with a vision, but without a rigid pricing structure to anchor your development, you lose focus, burn through hours, and eventually, the client loses faith. It is not a coding problem; it is a project management failure disguised as technical debt.

But there is a way out. By shifting your approach to treat pricing as a technical constraint, you transform your workflow from a never-ending slog into a high-velocity production line. With the right tools—Antigravity for architectural clarity and Claude Code for rapid execution—you can stop spinning your wheels and start shipping production-ready SaaS products that actually reach the finish line.

steps.title: Define Tiered Value Constraints
steps.description: Establish three pricing tiers. This forces you to define exactly what 'done' looks like for each level, preventing infinite feature expansion.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the core value proposition of my SaaS idea and suggest three distinct pricing tiers. Define the MVP feature set for each tier to ensure the 'Basic' tier is shippable in under 20 hours.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a markdown spec sheet based on these three pricing tiers'

steps.title: Scaffold the Core Architecture
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate a clean, modular foundation that aligns with your pricing tiers, ensuring you don't over-engineer the base layer.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a scalable SaaS architecture using Next.js, Supabase, and Stripe. Focus on a modular design where features can be toggled based on the pricing tier defined in step 1.

steps.title: Automate Stripe Integration
steps.description: Implement payment logic early. If the code for the pricing model is in place, the project scope becomes physically locked to your pricing tiers.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a robust Stripe webhook handler and backend service that updates user access levels based on the pricing tier selected during checkout.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement stripe-webhooks.ts and ensure user roles update in the database'

steps.title: Rapid Iterative Component Injection
steps.description: Use Claude Code to build out features one by one, strictly adhering to the tier requirements. Avoid touching code outside the current scope.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a reusable 'FeatureGate' component that hides or shows UI elements based on the user's active subscription tier.
steps.claude_code_command: claude code --write 'Add FeatureGate component to protect premium routes'

steps.title: Final QA and Deployment Prep
steps.description: Run an automated audit to ensure no 'hidden features' have crept into the codebase, then prepare for deployment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the current codebase against the original project scope. Identify any unused code or over-engineered modules that should be pruned before launch.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Audit the codebase for scope creep and suggest cleanup actions'

pro_tips: Always price your tiers based on the client's ROI, not your hours; it changes the client's perception of value immediately.
Use 'Feature Flags' as a technical implementation of your pricing tiers; if it's not in the tier, the code shouldn't even be accessible.
Never start coding until the Stripe product IDs are mapped to specific feature sets in your documentation.
When a client asks for a new feature, point to the pricing tier document and offer a 'Tier Upgrade' rather than 'Free Work'.
Use Claude Code to generate your unit tests based on the pricing constraints; this ensures your code remains 'done' and doesn't regress.
content.next_steps: Once the MVP is deployed, gather feedback from your first three paying users to refine your pricing tiers before scaling your feature set.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting projects stall in the 'almost-done' phase. Master the art of pricing-driven development today. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
